fresh_facts.claim: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth reported the lowest number of Iranian missile and drone attacks in any 24-hour period since the war began during a Tuesday Pentagon briefing.
U.S. Central Command reported over 12,300 targets hit, more than 155 Iranian vessels damaged or destroyed, and 13,000 combat flights flown as of Wednesday.
Two additional MQ-9 Reaper drones were lost near Isfahan, bringing the total U.S. drone losses to 16 according to U.S. officials.
Israel carried out a wide-scale wave of strikes on Tehran infrastructure sites on Wednesday, with Iranian state broadcaster IRIB reporting blasts in several areas.
Iran's military central command Khatam Al-Anbiya announced a new wave of missile and drone attacks targeting Israeli cities and U.S. facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait on Wednesday.
The IRGC claimed to have downed a U.S. MQ-9 drone over Isfahan on Monday.
Thousands of U.S. soldiers from the 82nd Airborne Division and an amphibious ready group with embarked Marines are staged in the Middle East region.
President Trump stated operations could end in 2-3 weeks and planned a prime-time update Wednesday night on military operations in Iran.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Trump statement

fresh_facts.claim: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian stated Tuesday that Iran has the necessary will to end the war with security guarantees.
Mojtaba Khamenei succeeded his father as Supreme Leader and issued his first statement demanding U.S. Middle East base closures and keeping the Strait closed as leverage.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Iranian succession

fresh_facts.claim: Iran appointed Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, a hardline ex-IRGC deputy, as Supreme National Security Council chief, replacing Ali Larijani.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Iranian appointments

fresh_facts.claim: The U.S. delivered a 15-point peace plan to Iran via Pakistan covering Strait of Hormuz reopening, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback.
Iran agreed to release 20 cargo ships through the Strait of Hormuz, which President Trump characterized as a negotiation win.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Trump statements

fresh_facts.claim: U.S. officials indicated potential talks are set for Pakistan this weekend regarding the conflict.
Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf rejected U.S. talks on social media.
fresh_facts.source: Social media statement by Iranian official

fresh_facts.claim: U.S. B-52 bombers conducted overland bombings of Iranian defense factories, nuclear labs, and infrastructure as part of ongoing operations.
Trump threatened to strike Kharg Island power plants if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Trump statements

fresh_facts.claim: Trump claimed Iran requested a ceasefire, which Iran's foreign minister denied according to Al Jazeera reporting.
Israel struck Tehran's Taleghan nuclear compound again after detecting rebuild efforts.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Israeli military operations

fresh_facts.claim: According to an Institute for the Study of War report dated March 31, 2026, a combined U.S.-Israel campaign targeting Iranian commanders is likely hindering sizable, coordinated attacks.
fresh_facts.source: Institute for the Study of War

fresh_facts.claim: Recent protests in Iran represent the most serious domestic unrest in regime history, threatening its character.
Iran runs a multidomain campaign targeting energy, cyber, and maritime systems for coercion amid U.S.-Israeli strikes.
Trump expressed frustration with NATO allies not supporting war efforts and told them to obtain their own oil supplies.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Trump statements

fresh_facts.claim: Iranian officials warned of global attacks on adversaries including tourist centers as part of escalating rhetoric.
fresh_facts.source: Wire service reporting on Iranian statements

regime_fall.current_status: The Iranian regime faces a decisive military collapse with over 12,300 targets hit and 155+ vessels destroyed, while simultaneously experiencing a succession crisis with hardliner Mojtaba Khamenei appointing hardline security officials, yet mixed diplomatic signals emerge as both sides hint at negotiations while maintaining rhetorical escalation.
regime_fall.last_major_event: U.S. Central Command reported over 12,300 targets hit, 155+ Iranian vessels damaged or destroyed, and 13,000 combat flights flown as of April 1, 2026, while Iran announced new waves of missile attacks and appointed hardline security officials, signaling both military desperation and succession instability.
